title: Deepfake Scams: Protect Yourself Now
modules: Module 1: Real Cases — When Deepfakes Cost People Money and Reputation
Module 2: How Audio and Video Deepfakes Are Made — What You Need to Know
Module 3: Recognizing Deepfake Red Flags in Video Calls, Clips, and Voice Messages
Module 4: Verification Tactics — Confirming Identity Without Trusting Your Eyes
Module 5: Protecting Your Own Voice and Likeness from Being Cloned
Module 6: What to Do After a Deepfake Attack — Reporting and Recovery
Module 7: Family and Community Conversations — Spreading Awareness Without Panic
synopsis: Scammers are now cloning the voices of CEOs, grandchildren, and public officials in real time to extract money and secrets from people who trust what they hear and see. This course teaches everyday users how deepfakes are constructed, how to spot them under pressure, and how to build simple verification habits before trust and money are lost.
rationale: Deepfake audio and video tools have become cheap and accessible enough for low-sophistication criminals, yet public awareness of real-time voice cloning scams remains critically low.
learning_outcome: Learners can apply a personal identity-verification protocol during unexpected calls or video interactions and take immediate protective action when deepfake fraud is suspected.
source_signals: Deepfakes are flooding retailers as part of scammers' return fraud operations
New Scams to Watch Out For in 2025
How AI and deepfakes are reshaping identity fraud in 2026
